channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Notícias - Partido Novo
item.description: The Novo Party invites members to run as candidates in 2026, emphasizing its commitment to lower taxes, fighting censorship, supporting entrepreneurs, and respecting honest Brazilians. The party states its values align with the right and seeks to attract principled, autonomous individuals for electoral contests.

item.title: Lula volta a criticar Conselho de Segurança da ONU: “Omisso”
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized the UN Security Council for being 'omissive' in resolving global conflicts, despite acknowledging the organization's historic achievements in decolonization, human rights, and disease eradication. He delivered the remarks during the opening of COP15 in Campo Grande, MS, warning that a world without rules is unsafe. The event was attended by figures including Paraguayan President Santiago Peña and Brazilian ministers, while Minister Sonia Guajajara was hospitalized and unable to attend.

item.title: Nova área protegida, ampliação de conservação e proteção: entenda os decretos assinados por Lula na COP15 em MS
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva announced at COP15 in Campo Grande the expansion of protected areas in the Pantanal and the creation of a new conservation unit in the Cerrado, totaling over 174,000 hectares. The measures include expanding the Pantanal Matogrossense National Park and the Taiamã Ecological Station, plus establishing the Córregos dos Vales do Norte de Minas Sustainable Development Reserve, aiming to protect biodiversity, water resources, and traditional communities.

item.title: Na COP15, Lula assina decretos para ampliar áreas de conservação
item.description: President Lula signed decrees at COP15 to expand conservation areas in Brazil, including 47,000 hectares in Pantanal Matogrossense National Park and 56,959 hectares in Taiamã Ecological Station, plus the creation of the Córregos dos Vales do Norte de Minas Sustainable Development Reserve. Investments total R$ 824.95 million, aiming to protect biodiversity, water resources, and curb illegal deforestation.

item.title: Lula afirma que Conselho de Segurança da ONU tem sido omisso na busca por soluções de conflitos
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized the UN Security Council for being passive in seeking solutions to international conflicts, stating that the body is failing to fulfill its role in maintaining global peace and security. He made the remarks on Sunday, the 22nd, during a public event, emphasizing the need for reform in the organization’s power structures.

item.title: Na COP15, Lula chama Conselho de Segurança da ONU de "omisso"
item.description: At COP15 in Campo Grande (MS), President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized the UN Security Council for being "omiss" in resolving global conflicts, blaming unilateral actions and the veto power of its five permanent members (US, China, Russia, France, UK) for undermining multilateralism. He called for a rules-based world, welcoming policies, and a renewed collective security system, stressing that these five nations — the world’s top arms producers and nuclear powers — have a duty to uphold peace. Lula linked current geopolitical tensions to the UN’s failure to prevent wars and protect human rights, migrants, and refugees.

item.title: CPMI do INSS quer prorrogação para não terminar em fracasso absoluto
item.description: The INSS CPMI, established in August to investigate fraud against retirees, risks ending without significant results after seven months of work. Although it uncovered fraud schemes involving associations, unions, and lobbyist 'Careca do INSS,' it failed to deepen investigations into key figures such as Frei Chico (President Lula's brother), Lulinha (the president's eldest son), lawyer Paulo Boudens, and Senator Weverton Rocha, nor did it advance on promised consigned credit fraud inquiries. Lawmakers requested an extension from Congress, which was denied, and now appeal to the STF, with the decision resting on Minister André Mendonça.

item.title: Lula destaca importância do Pantanal na abertura da COP15 em MS
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva inaugurated COP15 in Campo Grande, emphasizing the Pantanal’s role as a migratory species corridor and announcing the expansion of Pantanal Matogrossense National Park and Taiamã Ecological Station, plus the creation of the Córregos dos Vales do Norte de Minas Sustainable Development Reserve. Environment Minister Marina Silva opened the summit, stressing the need to align economic development with environmental protection and warning of threats such as habitat loss, climate change, and invasive species. Lula stated that Mato Grosso do Sul was strategically chosen as the gateway to the Pantanal, a vital biome for international biodiversity.

item.title: Lula volta a criticar Conselho de Segurança da ONU e chama órgão de omisso
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva once again criticized the UN Security Council, calling it 'omissive,' during a speech in Campo Grande at the COP15. This marked his second criticism in two days, following remarks at the Celac forum in Colombia. Lula argued the body fails to act in the face of global conflicts and humanitarian crises.

item.title: Imagem internacional do Brasil na área ambiental enfrentava questionamentos, diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized the previous Jair Bolsonaro government, stating that Brazil's international environmental image was being questioned due to environmental policies adopted during that period. He made these remarks during the 15th Conference of the Parties to the UN Convention on Migratory Species (COP15), held in Campo Grande, Mato Grosso do Sul.

item.title: Premiê esloveno reivindica vitória eleitoral; resultados apontam empate técnico
item.description: Slovenian Prime Minister Robert Golob claimed victory in Sunday’s parliamentary elections, although official results show a technical tie with conservative leader Janez Jansa, an admirer of Donald Trump. The contest reflects growing political polarization in Slovenia, with implications for future governability.

item.title: Socialista vence disputa em Paris e mantém capital sob comando da esquerda
item.description: Socialist Emmanuel Grégoire was elected mayor of Paris with approximately 52% of the vote, defeating conservative Rachida Dati and keeping the city under left-wing leadership for another term. His victory was seen as a clear rebuke to the far-right, especially after far-right candidate Sarah Knafo endorsed Dati in an effort to unite opposition forces. Grégoire emphasized the symbolic weight of the win and looked ahead to next year’s presidential election, anticipating a fiercely contested race.

item.title: Marina Silva tem pico de pressão durante a COP15
item.description: Environment and Climate Change Minister Marina Silva experienced a blood pressure spike during the UN's COP15 conference on migratory species, according to her ministry's press office. She was attending a special session alongside President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in Campo Grande. Officials stated her pressure rose slightly but she is now in good condition.

item.title: "Paris nunca será de extrema direita", diz novo prefeito da cidade
item.description: Emmanuel Grégoire, the Socialist Party candidate, won the 2026 Paris mayoral election with 53.1% of the vote, defeating Rachida Dati of the Republicans and Sophia Chikirou of La France Insoumise. In his victory speech, he firmly rejected the far right, declaring Paris would never be an extremist-right city, and celebrated a progressive, ecological, and inclusive vision for the capital while calling for voter unity.

item.title: Na procura por um vice, Flávio achou 2 adversários na direita
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro lost the support of Romeu Zema and Ratinho Júnior as potential running mates, who chose to run independently instead. This turns two potential allies into direct rivals, further fragmenting the Bolsonarist right. His strategy to unify the conservative bloc suffered a major setback.

item.title: O fantasma da Lava-Jato e a postura de André Mendonça no caso Master
item.description: Supreme Court Justice André Mendonça, rapporteur of the Banco Master inquiry, sparked conflict with Prosecutor-General Paulo Gonet by granting only 24 hours to comment on a new arrest of banker Daniel Vorcaro, leading Gonet to abstain and undermining plea deal negotiations. Mendonça also held private meetings with federal police without the MP’s presence, reviving concerns of bias reminiscent of the Lava-Jato era and Sergio Moro’s conduct, while stating he aims to avoid electoral turbulence in 2026. A potential plea bargain by Vorcaro could implicate top officials, ministers, and congressmembers, but its success hinges on verifiable evidence, given past annulments of unsubstantiated collaborations.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula fala na sessão de alto nível da COP15, em Campo Grande
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva spoke at the opening of the high-level session of COP15 in Campo Grande, marking the first time Brazil hosts and presides over the Convention on Migratory Species. During the event, Lula launched the Pantanal Declaration, aiming to expand participation in the convention — currently with 133 countries — and outlined goals for Brazil’s three-year presidency, including increasing funding and launching a research grant. The opening was attended by Brazilian ministers, indigenous leaders, and international officials, including Paraguayan President Santiago Peña and Bolivian Chancellor Fernando Aramayo Carrasco.

item.title: Vasco bate o Grêmio em casa e emplaca terceira vitória em quatro jogos no Brasileirão
item.description: Vasco da Gama defeated Grêmio 2-1 at home in the eighth round of the 2026 Brasileirão, with goals from Thiago Mendes (own goal) and David. It was Vasco's third win in four games under Renato Gaúcho, bringing them to 11 points and closer to the G-4 zone.

item.title: Nunes critica saída de Tebet do MDB: “Marionete de Lula”
item.description: São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes (MDB) publicly criticized Planning Minister Simone Tebet’s decision to leave the MDB and run for Senate in São Paulo under the PSB banner, calling her a 'puppet of Lula'. Nunes said he never expected someone of Tebet’s stature to become an instrument of President Lula. He also questioned the motives behind her party switch, suggesting it aligns with federal government interests.

item.title: Renan Santos chama Flávio de “retardado” por dancinhas
item.description: Pre-candidate Renan Santos (Missão) criticized Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) by calling him a 'complete mentally retarded' in an X post, accompanied by a video showing Bolsonaro performing a dance during an event in João Pessoa (PB). The dance, used as a strategy to soften the Bolsonaro family's image, had previously appeared in events in Rio de Janeiro and Rondônia.

item.title: Vanderlei Luxemburgo recebe alta do hospital e brinca: ‘Enverga, mas não quebra’
item.description: Coach Vanderlei Luxemburgo was discharged from the hospital after treatment for a lung infection and joked about his resilience, saying he's 'like a quince branch, it bends but doesn't break'. He has been away from football since 2023 and was recently named by Podemos as a pre-candidate for the Federal Senate from Tocantins in the 2026 elections.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro passa a fazer dancinha em comícios em novo estilo
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), pre-candidate for President, has started incorporating dance moves into pre-campaign events as a strategy to soften the image of the Bolsonaro family. The senator performed the dance at events such as the PL affiliation of Senator Efraim Filho in João Pessoa (PB) and the launch of Marcos Rogério’s gubernatorial pre-candidacy in Rondônia, set to the song 'Zero Um, Novo Capitão'. The move drew criticism, including from Renan Santos (Missão), who called him a 'complete mental retard'.

item.title: Esquerda mantém Paris e Marselha em eleições municipais na França
item.description: The moderate left retained control of Paris and Marseille in France's 2026 municipal elections, with Emmanuel Grégoire winning Paris by 53.1% of the vote. Turnout was around 57%, indicating voter disillusionment, while the far-right and radical left made gains in mid-sized cities. The results serve as a strength test ahead of the 2027 presidential election, in which Macron cannot run again.

item.title: Gusttavo Lima canta Hino Nacional na abertura do MotoGP em Goiânia. Veja vídeo
item.description: Sertanejo singer Gusttavo Lima performed the Brazilian National Anthem at the opening of the Brazilian MotoGP Grand Prix in Goiânia, marking the series' return to Brazil after over two decades. His helicopter arrival and vocal performance with a violão accompaniment moved the crowd, drawing applause and attended by officials including Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado and sports figures Felipe Massa and Cafu.

item.title: Conservadores ganham eleições regionais na Alemanha
item.description: The conservative CDU won regional elections in Rhineland-Palatinate with 30.5% of the vote, ahead of the SPD (between 26.5% and 26.9%), ending the latter’s 35-year rule. The far-right AfD more than doubled its 2021 result, reaching 20% and becoming the state’s third-largest party. CDU candidate Gordon Schneider celebrated the victory as a 'return,' citing voter demand for change in education, security, health, and economic policy.

item.title: França: socialista Emmanuel Grégoire é eleito novo prefeito de Paris, apontam projeções
item.description: Socialist Emmanuel Grégoire was elected mayor of Paris with 53.1% of the vote in the second round of municipal elections, according to initial projections. In Marseille, Benoît Payan of the Diverse Left coalition was re-elected with 54.6%. The 2026 French municipal elections showed strong left-wing performance in major cities.

item.title: Em pré-campanha, Flávio Bolsonaro faz 1ª agenda no Nordeste
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) launched a pre-campaign tour in the Northeast, visiting Natal and João Pessoa, where he received support from União Brasil and opposition leaders. Speaking in a softer tone, he called the Northeast the 'solution' and criticized diesel prices, claiming it reached R$10 in RN without directly mentioning Lula. The tour takes place in a region historically favorable to the PT, focusing on economic and infrastructure issues.

item.title: Bolívia vai às urnas em clima de fragmentação política
item.description: Bolivia holds municipal and gubernatorial elections with over 7.5 million voters amid political fragmentation following 20 years of left-wing dominance. The Movement for Socialism is declining, while center-right candidate Rodrigo Paz gains traction, advocating for separating politics from economic outcomes amid the country's worst economic crisis in 40 years. Analysts describe a structural reorganization of the political landscape, with no single party hegemony remaining.

item.title: Ministra dos Povos Indígenas está internada em UTI em SP com suspeita de quadro infeccioso
item.description: Indigenous Peoples Minister Sonia Guajajara was admitted to the ICU at InCor in São Paulo with a suspected infectious condition, presenting high fever, abdominal pain, and general malaise. A PSOL member running for re-election as federal deputy for São Paulo, she must step down from her ministerial role by April 4. Her care is being managed by cardiologist Sérgio Timerman and infectologist Rinaldo Focaccia Siciliano.

item.title: Ministra Sonia Guajajara é internada em SP com suspeita de infecção
item.description: Indigenous Peoples Minister Sonia Guajajara (PSol) was admitted to the ICU at InCor-HCFMUSP in São Paulo due to suspected infection, presenting malaise, high fever, and abdominal pain. Her condition is stable with favorable clinical evolution, and no discharge date has been set. She confirmed she will leave office on March 30 to run for re-election as a federal deputy from São Paulo.

item.title: Quem é Mateus Simões, vice que assume lugar de Zema em MG
item.description: Romeu Zema resigned as governor of Minas Gerais on March 22, 2026, handing over power to his vice-governor, Mateus Simões, who will serve until December 2026. Simões, 45, is a native of Gurupi (TO), holds a law degree and a master's in Business Law, and previously served as Secretary-General of Government, prosecutor for the Legislative Assembly, and city councilor in Belo Horizonte. He was elected vice-governor in 2022 alongside Zema.

item.title: Flávio acusa Lula de tentar emplacar ‘narrativa falsa’ ao associar caso Master à direita
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro accused President Lula of spreading a "false narrative" by linking the Banco Master scandal to the right, advocating for a CPI and asserting that the scheme is tied to Lula's allies in Bahia, such as Jaques Wagner and Rui Costa. He also hopes Daniel Vorcaro will cooperate with a plea deal to reveal the full truth.

item.title: Janja mostra Lula fazendo exercício: “Domingo começou forte”
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva posted a video on Instagram showing President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, 80, exercising while wearing a Corinthians shorts. The Sunday (March 22, 2026) post aims to highlight Lula’s good health, a key concern in his re-election bid for a fourth term. He will turn 81 after the second round of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Cláudio Castro marca data para renunciar ao cargo; saiba quem assume o governo do RJ
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro will resign from office on Monday at 4:30 PM at Guanabara Palace, one day before the TSE resumes judgment that could render him ineligible for eight years. Without a vice governor, he will be replaced by Ricardo Couto, president of the TJ-RJ, who will call an indirect election in Alerj to choose the new governor. The resignation is a strategic move to nullify the TSE case and enable Castro to run for a Senate seat under the PL, with Douglas Ruas as the gubernatorial candidate.

item.title: Fechadas sessões eleitorais na França, resultado incerto em Paris
item.description: French municipal elections closed with 57% turnout, reflecting historical disinterest and serving as a barometer for the 2027 presidential race. Édouard Philippe won in Le Havre, while the left leads in Paris and Marseille, with LFI strong in cities like Roubaix and Saint-Denis. The far right failed to gain ground in Nîmes and Toulon, and abstention was the second highest since 2020.

item.title: Zema renuncia e critica governo Lula: “Somos um país roubado”
item.description: Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) resigned from office in Minas Gerais on Sunday (22), handing power to his vice-governor Mateus Simões (PSD). Zema, who is positioning himself as a pre-candidate for president in 2026, sharply criticized the Lula administration during the ceremony, claiming Brazil is being 'robbed' and that 'no one can tolerate the revelry anymore'. His resignation is part of a strategy to strengthen his national presidential bid.

item.title: INSS: CPMI vai pedir indiciamento de 200 pessoas em relatório oficial
item.description: The final report of the INSS CPI is expected to recommend the indictment of over 200 people, including members of the Lula government, spanning more than 5,000 pages prepared by rapporteur Alfredo Gaspar (União Brasil-AL). The document will be presented on March 25 and voted on March 26, the commission's last day, as government allies prepare an alternative 800-page report.

item.title: Zema deixa governo de Minas e abre caminho para corrida presidencial
item.description: Romeu Zema formally resigned as governor of Minas Gerais to dedicate himself fully to his presidential pre-campaign under the Novo party, anticipating an expected political move. His vice-governor, Mateus Simões, assumed the state executive in a ceremony at the ALMG and Palácio da Liberdade, positioning himself as a potential candidate for the Minas Gerais governorship in 2026. Zema ruled out alliances with figures like Flávio Bolsonaro or traditional right-wing parties, signaling an independent presidential bid.

item.title: Ministra Sônia Guajajara é internada em UTI em São Paulo
item.description: Indigenous Peoples Minister Sônia Guajajara (PSOL) was hospitalized in the ICU at InCor-HCFMUSP for investigation of an infectious condition, presenting high fever, abdominal pain, and malaise, with favorable clinical evolution according to an official statement. She announced she will leave office on March 30 to run for re-election as a federal deputy for São Paulo, with Eloy Terena expected to assume the ministry. Her case is being monitored by cardiologist Sérgio Timerman and infectologist Rinaldo Focaccia Siciliano.

item.title: Partido do chanceler alemăo lidera eleiçőes regionais; extrema direita avança
item.description: The conservative CDU party, led by German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is leading the regional elections in Rhineland-Palatinate. The far-right may achieve a historic vote record, according to initial estimates released on Sunday (22).

item.title: Servidores protestam em MG: "Zema + Simões é igual trem desgovernado"
item.description: State public servants in Minas Gerais protested in Belo Horizonte against the government of Romeu Zema and Vice Governor Mateus Simões, labeling the administration a 'runaway train'. The demonstration coincided with Simões' inauguration as governor, following Zema's resignation to focus on a presidential pre-candidacy. Protesters denounced the deterioration of public services and lack of career valorization, with participation from teachers, health and environmental workers.

item.title: Flávio critica Lula e defende classificar facções criminosas como terroristas
item.description: In João Pessoa, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, the PL's pré-candidate for president, criticized President Lula for alleged inaction against organized crime and said he would classify criminal factions as terrorist organizations if elected. He also dismissed claims of a possible U.S. intervention led by Trump against Lula's government as a lie and an attempt to spread terror due to the PT's incompetence.

item.title: Público canta parabéns para Michelle Bolsonaro, e Flávio exalta a ex-primeira-dama
item.description: During a pre-campaign event for Flávio Bolsonaro in João Pessoa, the public sang 'happy birthday' to Michelle Bolsonaro, who was also praised by the senator on social media. Flávio emphasized Michelle's role in the political landscape, downplaying the lack of support for his own presidential bid. The content includes links and excerpts from other UOL stories, but the central focus is the celebration of Michelle Bolsonaro's birthday.

item.title: Flávio: ladrăo vai mofar na cadeia e será neutralizado se enfrentar polícia
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro reiterated hardline security proposals, stating that criminals 'will rot in jail' and will be 'neutralized' if they confront police — a term associated with killing suspects in confrontations. He also advocated lowering the age of criminal responsibility to 16 (14 for rapists) and attacked Lula, accusing him of enabling crime and being soft on criminals. The remarks were made at rallies in João Pessoa and Natal, reinforcing a radical stance to gain public support amid perceptions of ineffective current policies.

item.title: Gleisi volta a criticar Selic e classifica taxa do rotativo do cartão de crédito de ‘criminosa’
item.description: Brazil's Minister of Institutional Relations, Gleisi Hoffmann (PT), criticized the Selic rate as 'shameful' and credit card revolving interest as 'criminal', arguing that high interest rates are undermining President Lula's gains in employment and income, burdening families and workers. She called for a 'civic crusade' against the current Selic level, describing the Central Bank's 0.25 percentage point cut as disappointing and insufficient amid global inflationary pressures.

item.title: PSDB lança ex-assessor do governo FHC para Câmara
item.description: The PSDB has nominated Raul Christiano, a former advisor to Fernando Henrique Cardoso’s government and one of the party’s founders, as its federal deputy candidate for São Paulo, targeting voters in the Baixada Santista region. The move aims to restore the party’s political relevance after a poor showing in the 2024 municipal elections, where it elected only two mayors in cities over 200,000 voters, and following the defection of six state deputies to the PSD.

item.title: Paes critica Cláudio Castro por deixar governo do Rio: “Governador omisso fugindo da justiça”
item.description: Eduardo Paes, a pre-candidate for Rio de Janeiro governor, criticized Governor Cláudio Castro for resigning one day before a TSE session that could render him ineligible, accusing him of being an 'omissive governor fleeing justice' and using a legal maneuver to evade responsibility for alleged crimes. Paes stated the resignation is an attempt to avoid ineligibility and that the TSE will not accept such tactics, noting Castro aims to name a successor to continue his political project.

item.title: Lula e Petro defendem fortalecimento do multilateralismo regional
item.description: Lula and Petro advocated for strengthening regional multilateralism during a meeting in Bogotá, focusing on the transition of Celac's presidency to Uruguay and promoting democracy against extremism. Lula also met with Burundi's president, celebrating the country's entry into the Global Alliance against Hunger and Poverty and highlighting Embrapa's expansion in Africa with a new unit in Ethiopia. There was a divergence on UN succession: Brazil supports Michelle Bachelet for the first Latin American female leadership, while Burundi backs Macky Sall.

item.title: Sônia Guajajara está internada em UTI no InCor, em SP, com suspeita de quadro infeccioso
item.description: Indigenous Peoples Minister Sônia Guajajara is hospitalized in the ICU at InCor in São Paulo for investigation of an infectious condition, with favorable clinical evolution and stable vital signs. She announced she will leave the ministry on March 30 to run for re-election as a federal deputy from São Paulo.

item.title: Flávio acena a Michelle em aniversário e tenta conter falta de apoio na pré-campanha
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) made a public gesture of support for former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro on her birthday, aiming to quell rumors of distancing in the presidential pre-campaign. While allies consider her support likely, Michelle remains discreet, focused on family and accompanying Jair Bolsonaro, with no defined intensity of involvement. The PL views her as a strategic asset to improve performance among female voters but pressures her for a clearer stance as the electoral calendar advances.
